---
title: "12_report_figures_light_theme"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 1. Load packages
#--------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(pROC)
library(forcats)

dir.create(here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(here("outputs", "tables"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 2. Report colour theme
#--------------------------------------------------

theme_report <- function(base_size = 13) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#E6DFF5", linewidth = 0.35),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
      plot.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold", size = base_size + 4),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "#4B3B66", size = base_size),
      legend.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
      strip.text = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"))
}

purple_source <- c(
  "Expression" = "#C4B5FD",
  "Methylation" = "#8B5CF6",
  "Multiomic" = "#111111")

purple_model <- c(
  "Elastic Net" = "#C4B5FD",
  "Random Forest" = "#111111")

purple_class <- c(
  "No" = "#111111",
  "Yes" = "#A78BFA")
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 3. Load RDS files for class balance
#--------------------------------------------------

expr_data <- read_rds(
  here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_expression_with_outcome.rds"))

meth_data <- read_rds(
  here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_methylation_with_outcome.rds"))

multi_data <- read_rds(
  here("data", "interim", "merged_clinical_multiomic_with_outcome.rds"))

class_balance <- bind_rows(
  expr_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(survival_500)) %>%
    dplyr::count(survival_500, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(dataset = "Expression"),
  
  meth_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(survival_500)) %>%
    dplyr::count(survival_500, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(dataset = "Methylation"),
  
  multi_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(survival_500)) %>%
    dplyr::count(survival_500, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(dataset = "Multiomic")) %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("Expression", "Methylation", "Multiomic")),
    survival_500 = factor(survival_500, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  )

class_balance
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 4. Figure: class balance
#--------------------------------------------------

p_class_balance <- class_balance %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = dataset, y = n, fill = survival_500)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.68) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    vjust = -0.35,
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = purple_class) +
  labs(
    title = "500-day survival class balance by cohort",
    subtitle = "Labelled patients used in each modelling dataset",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Survival > 500 days") +
  theme_report(base_size = 13)

p_class_balance

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_class_balance_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_class_balance,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 5. Load final model comparison table
#--------------------------------------------------

final_comparison <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "final_model_comparison_extended.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    model = recode(
      model,
      "ElasticNet" = "Elastic Net",
      "RandomForest" = "Random Forest"),
    data_source = factor(data_source, levels = c("Expression", "Methylation", "Multiomic")))
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 6. Figure: CV ROC model comparison
#--------------------------------------------------

p_model_comparison <- final_comparison %>%
  mutate(
    model_label = paste(data_source, model),
    model_label = fct_reorder(model_label, ROC)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ROC, y = model_label, fill = data_source)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", ROC)),
    hjust = -0.15,
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = purple_source) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(final_comparison$ROC, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-validated ROC by model",
    subtitle = "Models ranked using repeated cross-validation ROC",
    x = "CV ROC",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Data source") +
  theme_report(base_size = 13)

p_model_comparison

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_model_comparison_cv_roc_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_model_comparison,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 7. Load saved CV predictions for ROC curves
#--------------------------------------------------

expr_preds <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "expression_cv_predictions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "Expression")

meth_preds <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "methylation_cv_predictions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "Methylation")

multi_preds <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "multiomic_cv_predictions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(data_source = "Multiomic")

rf_preds <- bind_rows(expr_preds, meth_preds, multi_preds) %>%
  mutate(
    model = recode(
      model,
      "RandomForest" = "Random Forest",
      "ElasticNet" = "Elastic Net")) %>%
  filter(model == "Random Forest") %>%
  mutate(model_label = paste(data_source, "Random Forest"))
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 8. Figure: ROC curves for Random Forest models
#--------------------------------------------------

roc_df <- rf_preds %>%
  group_by(model_label) %>%
  group_modify(~ {
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = .x$obs,
      predictor = .x$Yes,
      levels = c("No", "Yes"),
      direction = "<")
    tibble(
      specificity = roc_obj$specificities,
      sensitivity = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

p_roc <- roc_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, colour = model_label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.15) +
  geom_abline(
    intercept = 0,
    slope = 1,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "#999999") +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Expression Random Forest" = "#C4B5FD",
      "Methylation Random Forest" = "#8B5CF6",
      "Multiomic Random Forest" = "#111111")) +
  labs(
    title = "ROC curves for random forest models",
    subtitle = "Sensitivity and false positive rate across classification thresholds",
    x = "False positive rate",
    y = "True positive rate",
    colour = "Model") +
  theme_report(base_size = 13)

p_roc

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_roc_curves_random_forest_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_roc,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 9. Load sensitivity analysis results
#--------------------------------------------------

sensitivity_results <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "sensitivity_analysis_results.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 10. Figure: sensitivity analysis
#--------------------------------------------------

p_sensitivity <- sensitivity_results %>%
  mutate(
    scenario_label = case_when(
      scenario == "feature_count" ~ paste0(feature_count, " probes; 5×5 CV"),
      scenario == "cv_design" ~ paste0("2,000 probes; ", folds, "×", repeats, " CV"),
      TRUE ~ scenario),
    scenario_label = fct_reorder(scenario_label, ROC)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ROC, y = scenario_label, fill = scenario)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", ROC)),
    hjust = -0.15,
    colour = "black",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "feature_count" = "#8B5CF6",
      "cv_design" = "#111111")) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(sensitivity_results$ROC, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.12)) +
  labs(
    title = "Methylation random forest sensitivity analysis",
    subtitle = "Robustness across feature counts and cross-validation designs",
    x = "CV ROC",
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL) +
  theme_report(base_size = 13)

p_sensitivity

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_sensitivity_analysis_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_sensitivity,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 11. Load methylation annotation table
#--------------------------------------------------

top_methylation_report_table <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "top_methylation_report_table.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 12. Figure: top methylation feature importance
#--------------------------------------------------

p_meth_importance <- top_methylation_report_table %>%
  mutate(
    label = ifelse(Gene == "—", Probe, paste0(Gene, " (", Probe, ")")),
    label = fct_reorder(label, Importance)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Importance, y = label)) +
  geom_segment(
    aes(x = 0, xend = Importance, y = label, yend = label),
    colour = "#8B5CF6",
    linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_point(
    colour = "#111111",
    fill = "#C4B5FD",
    size = 4,
    shape = 21,
    stroke = 1.1) +
  labs(
    title = "Top methylation random forest features",
    subtitle = "Variable importance mapped to annotated probes",
    x = "Importance",
    y = NULL) +
  theme_report(base_size = 13)

p_meth_importance

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_top_methylation_importance_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_meth_importance,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# Confusion matrix plot: Multiomic Random Forest
#--------------------------------------------------

library(caret)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

multi_preds <- read_csv(
  here("outputs", "tables", "multiomic_cv_predictions.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE)

multi_rf_preds <- multi_preds %>%
  filter(model %in% c("RandomForest", "Random Forest"))

multi_rf_cm <- confusionMatrix(
  data = factor(multi_rf_preds$pred, levels = c("Yes", "No")),
  reference = factor(multi_rf_preds$obs, levels = c("Yes", "No")),
  positive = "Yes")

multi_rf_cm_table <- as.data.frame(multi_rf_cm$table)

names(multi_rf_cm_table) <- c("Predicted", "Actual", "Count")

p_multi_rf_cm <- multi_rf_cm_table %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Count)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), colour = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 7) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#C4B5FD",
    high = "#111111") +
  labs(
    title = "Confusion matrix: Multiomic Random Forest",
    subtitle = "Default classification threshold; Yes = survival beyond 500 days",
    x = "Actual class",
    y = "Predicted class",
    fill = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold", size = 17),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "#4B3B66"),
    legend.title = element_text(colour = "black", face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(colour = "black"))

p_multi_rf_cm

ggsave(
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "figure_confusion_matrix_multiomic_rf_light_purple.png"),
  plot = p_multi_rf_cm,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
#--------------------------------------------------
# 13. Save list of generated report figures
#--------------------------------------------------

report_figure_manifest <- tibble(
  figure = c(
    "Class balance",
    "Model comparison CV ROC",
    "ROC curves Random Forest",
    "Sensitivity analysis",
    "Top methylation importance"),
  file = c(
    "figure_class_balance_light_purple.png",
    "figure_model_comparison_cv_roc_light_purple.png",
    "figure_roc_curves_random_forest_light_purple.png",
    "figure_sensitivity_analysis_light_purple.png",
    "figure_top_methylation_importance_light_purple.png"))

write_csv(
  report_figure_manifest,
  here("outputs", "figures", "report_theme", "report_figure_manifest.csv"))

report_figure_manifest
```
